hub_subtitle: Business English para as reuniões com o time Global da Sanofi, congressos médicos e a apresentação de agosto

lesson.menu_title: Better, Bigger, Best -- Comparing Results in the Business Review
lesson.menu_desc: A hora da apresentação: comparar o Brasil com a região, uma área com a outra, este ano com o passado -- "Brazil is bigger than Mexico, but the growth is higher" -- sem travar -- 35 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 8 -- Better, Bigger, Best
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Emmanuele Orrico | Lesson 8 -- Better, Bigger, Best

listenings.text: Emmanuele, Marc Dubois. One message before the business review on Thursday. When you present Brazil, please do not give me a long list of numbers. Give me comparisons. I want to hear three things. First: how does Brazil compare to the region? Is it bigger or smaller than Mexico, and is the growth higher or lower? Second: what is your most important priority, and why is it the biggest opportunity? And third: where is the widest gap, the place with the most work to do? Remember, a number alone says nothing. The best review is the one with the clearest comparisons. See you Thursday.

Good morning, everyone. Claire Bennett here, with the quarterly comparison across the regions. First, the headline. Latin America has the highest growth of all the regions this quarter, and Brazil is the biggest market inside it. That is very good news. Now, the interesting part. The growth in Asia is a little lower, but the gap between the public and the private market is much smaller there, so the opportunity is different. My message is simple. Do not only look at the biggest number. Look at the trend. The region with the fastest growth this year is not the biggest one. It is one of the smallest. So when you send me your review, I do not want a long list. I want three comparisons and one clear priority. Thank you, everyone.

extra_audio.text: Good morning, Marc. Here is the comparison for Brazil. Brazil is the biggest market in Latin America, and this year it has the best performance. Mexico has a higher growth rate, but the gap is smaller than last year. Our most important priority is atopic dermatitis, the biggest opportunity. In my opinion, that is the most significant trend of the quarter.

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: the part of the whole market that one company has
a number in a report or a chart
the product, team or country that is number one
how well a product, a team or a market works and sells
the way the numbers change over time, up or down
a large area, like a group of countries
the difference, or the empty space, between two numbers
when something becomes better than before

paras: In a business review, every manager brings numbers. But a number alone says nothing. "We have twelve thousand patients" -- is that good? Nobody knows, until you compare it. Twelve thousand is a big figure if last year you had eight thousand. It is a small figure if your neighbor has fifty thousand. The number becomes a story only next to another number.

This is why a good review is full of comparisons. Brazil is bigger than Mexico, but the growth in Mexico is higher. Atopic dermatitis is our most important priority, but asthma has the best performance this quarter. The gap between the public and the private market is the widest in the region. Each sentence puts two things side by side, and the Global team understands in one second.

And there is one more rule, the hardest one: bigger is not always better. The biggest market is not always the best opportunity. Sometimes the smallest region has the fastest growth and the widest gap to close -- and that is exactly where the best opportunity is. A good manager does not only show the biggest number. She shows where the trend is going, and why the smallest number on the page can be the most significant one.

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: A business review should show only the biggest numbers, because a bigger number is always better.
A number means little alone. A good review compares numbers, and the biggest one is not always the best opportunity.
A manager should never compare Brazil to other countries in a global review.

items: The text says a single number, alone, tells the Global team everything they need.
The opposite: "a number alone says nothing." It becomes a story only next to another number.
According to the text, twelve thousand patients can be a big figure or a small one, depending on the comparison.
"a big figure if last year you had eight thousand... a small figure if your neighbor has fifty thousand."
The text says the biggest market is always the best opportunity.
It says the opposite: "bigger is not always better. The biggest market is not always the best opportunity."
According to the text, the smallest region can have the fastest growth and be the most significant one.
"the smallest region has the fastest growth... the smallest number on the page can be the most significant one."

growth rate. Atopic dermatitis is our

gapfill.text: Two families of adjectives. SHORT words -- one syllable: big, high, low, fast, small -- add -er and the -est: bigger, the biggest. LONG words -- two or more syllables: important, significant, expensive -- take more and the most: more important, the most important. And two words break every rule, so learn them by heart: good becomes better and best, bad becomes worse and worst.

lf.text: A and C use SHORT words -- big, high -- so they change their end: bigger, higher, and always with THAN. B uses a LONG word -- important -- so it takes the most. D is irregular: good never becomes goodest, it becomes the best. In your first language, one single word can work for everything -- and that is exactly why English splits into two roads, and why this is worth five minutes of your life.

bank.label: Useful language for a business review
items: Brazil is the biggest market in Latin America.
Brazil is bigger than Mexico, but Mexico is growing faster.
Mexico has a higher growth rate than Brazil.
Atopic dermatitis is our most important priority this quarter.
Asthma has the best performance of the three areas.
The gap is smaller than it was last year.
In my opinion, this is the most significant trend.

items.situation: Marc asks: "How does Brazil compare to Mexico in size?"
tips: Brazil is the biggest market in Latin America.

items.situation: "And the growth rate -- who is higher?"
tips: Mexico has a higher growth rate than Brazil.
The growth in Mexico is higher, but the gap is smaller than last year.

items.situation: "What is your most important priority right now?"
tips: Atopic dermatitis is our most important priority this quarter.
Our most important priority is atopic dermatitis -- it is the biggest opportunity.

items.situation: "Which therapeutic area has the best performance?"
tips: Asthma has the best performance of the three areas.
This quarter, asthma has the best performance.

items.situation: "What is your biggest challenge right now?"
tips: The public market is our biggest challenge.
Our biggest gap is the public market -- but it is also the best opportunity.

items.situation: Marc wants your opinion on the trend. Give it.
tips: In my opinion, this is the most significant trend of the year.
In my opinion, the trend is very positive: the numbers are better than last quarter.
